sections.paragraph: and צָו m., a precept (from the root צָוָה; like קַו, תָּו from the roots קָוָה, תָּוָה). Hos 5:11, הָלַךְ אַחֲרֵי צָֽו “he follows the precept” (of men), unless, indeed we should read שָׁו for שָׁוְא. Hence, Isa 28:10, 13 Isa 28:13, צַו לָעָו צַו לָצָו “precept upon precept, precept upon precept” (Lehr’ auf Lehr’ Lehr’ auf Lehr’), i.e. precept is added to precept, law to law, by the priests and prophets; we are daily wearied with new laws. The paronomasia is imitated by Jerome, manda, remanda, manda remanda.
